data.title: Accenture beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Accenture press release (NYSE:ACN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.03 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $17.6B (+7.3% Y/Y) beats by $240M.
  • New bookings of $21.3 billion for the quarter and $80.6 billion for the year.
  • Generative AI new bookings of $1.8 billion for the quarter and $5.9 billion for the year.
  • Adjusted1 operating margin of 15.1%, an increase of 10 basis points.
  • Free cash flow of $3.8 billion for the quarter and $10.9 billion for the year

    • Fiscal Year 2026 Business Outlook Highlights

    • Company expects full-year revenue growth of 2% to 5% in local currency vs. estimated growth of 5.28% Y/Y; excluding a 1% to 1.5% impact from its U.S. federal business, company expects revenue growth of 3% to 6% in local currency
    • Expects full-year GAAP diluted EPS of $13.19 to $13.57, a 9% to 12% increase; expects full-year adjusted EPS of $13.52 to $13.90 vs. consensus of $13.79, a 5% to 8% increase.
    • The company expects to return at least $9.3 billion in cash to shareholders in fiscal year 2026.
    • Q1 Revenue consensus of $18.52B

data.title: Archer Aviation sets an altitude record as its test flights continue
data.description: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE:ACHR) continues to work towards its goal to develop the key enabling technologies and aircraft necessary to power the future of aviation. The company announced earlier in the week that its Midnight aircraft successfully completed its highest altitude flight to date, reaching altitudes of 7,000 feet.The flight took off from Archer’s test facility in Salinas, California. During the flight, Midnight’s test pilot conducted a steady climb after takeoff, leveled off at altitude, then continued to fly for 45 miles in total at speeds exceeding 120 mph. The achievement follows last month’s record-setting flight in which Midnight covered approximately 55 miles in 31 minutes at speeds exceeding 126 mph.The company said the flight marked one in a series of upcoming aircraft performance test flights designed to test and showcase Midnight’s operational range. While eVTOL aircraft are primarily intended to operate in and around cities at altitudes between 1,500 and 4,000 feet. Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) noted that the operational capability to operate at higher altitudes provides flexibility for managing air traffic in high-density environments and allows the aircraft to be able to operate in certain cities at elevation."Our test pilots will continue to safely and methodically test Midnight’s speed, duration, and mission profiles to prepare for early commercial operations," stated CEO Adam Goldstein.

data.title: Capitol Hill intensifies scrutiny of tech H-1B usage amid layoffs- WSJ
data.description:
U.S. senators Chuck Grassley and Dick Durbin have sent out letters to tech giants Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN), Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), escalating their scrutiny of H-1B visas amid domestic layoffs, the Wall Street Journal reported Thursday.

Other firms receiving letters include Alphabet’s Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL), Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), Deloitte, Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Cognizant Technology Solutions (NASDAQ:CTSH), and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS). The companies have been asked to respond by October 10.

The lawmakers have reportedly asked for detailed information on the number of H-1B employees, their compensation, and any potential displacement of American workers, expressing concerns about the continued hiring of foreign workers amid layoffs.

This is the latest escalation from Washington, D.C. against the popular H-1B program, after President Donald Trump’s introduction of a $100,000 charge for H-1B visas.

Data from the Department of Homeland Security and U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services shows around 500,000 H-1B visa holders in the country, with India accounting for just over 70% in the 2024 fiscal year and China making up 12%.

data.title: TD SYNNEX beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q4 outlook
data.description:
• TD SYNNEX press release (NYSE:SNX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.58 beats by $0.53.
  • Revenue of $15.65B (+6.6% Y/Y) beats by $540M.
  • Non-GAAP gross billings of $22.7 billion, an increase of 12.1% year over year and above the high end of our outlook. On a constant currency basis, non-GAAP gross billings increased by 10.1% year over year.
  • Cash provided by operations of $246 million and free cash flow(1) of $214 million.
  • Announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.44 per common share, up 10% year over year.

data.title: Mytheresa - ADS GAAP EPS of €4.67, revenue of €248.9M; updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Mytheresa - ADS press release (NYSE:LUXE): Q4 GAAP EPS of €4.67.
  • Revenue of €248.9M (+11.5% Y/Y).
  • Significantly increased profitability for Mytheresa with an Adj. EBITDA margin of 6.5%, up 180bps vs. Q4 FY24.
  • GMV growth of 11.1% to €265.9 million in Q4 FY25 as compared to €239.3 million in the prior year period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of €16.1 million vs. €10.6 million in Q4 FY24 and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 6.5% in Q4 FY25 as compared to 4.7% in the prior year period.

  • GUIDANCE FY2026

    "For the topline, the Luxury | Mytheresa segment is expected to continue growing its GMV. The Luxury | NAP & MRP segment is expected to still slightly decline in GMV. The Off-price | YOOX & THE OUTNET segment will continue the restructuring of its operating and business model in FY26. We therefore expect GMV at Off-price | YOOX & THE OUTNET to continue to decrease considerably. In sum, LuxExperience at the Group level is expected to have a GMV at around €2.5-2.9 billion in FY26.

    Given the uncertainties in the market and FY26 being a transition year, we expect in FY26 comparable profitability levels to FY25. In sum, LuxExperience at Group level is expected to report an Adjusted EBITDA margin between (4%) and +1%."

    For FY26, LuxExperience therefore expects

    • GMV €2.5 billion to €2.9 billion and
    • an Adjusted EBITDA margin between (4%) to +1%

    LuxExperience reconfirms its medium-term annual growth rates of 10-15% and targets of €4 billion Net Sales with an underlying Adjusted EBITDA margin of 7% to 9%.

data.title: Robotruck firm Kodiak AI set to debut on Nasdaq after SPAC merger
data.description:
Kodiak Robotics is set to make its stock market debut on Thursday, following its merger with SPAC Ares Acquisition Corp. II (NYSE:AACT) that valued the autonomous trucking startup at about $2.5B.

The merged entity has been renamed Kodiak AI. Its stock and public warrants will start trading on Nasdaq under the tickers "KDK" and "KDKRW," respectively.

Under the SPAC deal, Kodiak received more than $212.5M from certain institutional investors, including $145M in PIPE funding and about $62.9M in trust cash from AACT, before expenses.

 

The company said its AI-powered autonomous driving system - Kodiak Driver - is designed to help address challenges in the trucking industry such as labor shortage, rising costs, and demand for shorter delivery timelines.

 

Kodiak Driver has logged approximately 3M on-road autonomous miles and over 3,000 paid driverless hours to date. Kodiak has partnered with vehicle upfitter Roush Industries to scale its production of autonomous trucks.

Kodiak's CEO Don Burnette told The Wall Street Journal that the company plans to use the funds raised from the SPAC deal to expand its driverless fleet and invest in R&D to improve autonomous capabilities.

Other autonomous trucking players include Aurora Innovation (AUR), Gatik, TuSimple, Plus, Torc, Volvo Autonomous Solutions, and Embark Truck.

data.title: CATL surpasses Kweichow Moutai to become China’s third-largest stock by market value
data.description:
• Contemporary Amperex Technology (CATL) overtook liquor maker Kweichow Moutai on Thursday to become China’s third-largest locally listed stock by market value, driven by a strong outlook for battery demand.
  • The Shenzhen-listed battery giant gained as much as 6% Thursday to a record high, pushing the company’s market capitalization to 1.83 trillion yuan ($257 billion), surpassing Moutai’s 1.8 trillion yuan valuation, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.
  • “Battery outlook is good,” and some investors may be rotating out of electric vehicle stocks into battery stocks, benefiting sector leader CATL, Kelvin Lau, an analyst at Daiwa Capital Markets Hong Kong, said.
  • CATL, the world’s largest electric vehicle battery manufacturer, saw its shares surge double digits on their Hong Kong debut in May following a record $4.6 billion IPO, boosted by high interest in EV battery suppliers.

data.title: Verbio SE reports FY results
data.description:
• Verbio SE press release (OTCPK:VBVBF): FY Revenue of €1.58B (-5.4% Y/Y).
  • Verbio confirms preliminary results with EBITDA of €14.2 million.
  • The increase in bioethanol and biomethane was primarily attributable to the plant in Nevada, Iowa (USA), whose production has risen continuously since the beginning of the year. The CO2 savings potential of the products produced and traded by Verbio also increased to 5.5 million tons of CO2 equivalent (FY 2023/24: 4.4 million tons).
  • The Executive Board expects EBITDA in the high double-digit million range for the 2025/26 fiscal year, significantly higher than the previous year.

data.title: BP launches £1.6 billion pension risk transfer deal with UK insurer L&G
data.description:
• UK insurer Legal and General Assurance Society on Thursday announced the completion of a £1.6 billion buy-in with the BP Pension Fund. 
  • Legal & General Group will manage risks for £1.6 billion ($2.2 billion) of BP’s (NYSE:BP) £18 billion fund, according to a statement on Thursday. 
  • The pension fund is among the UK’s largest and run by BP’s in-house asset manager.

data.title: KWS SAAT SE reports FY result
data.description:
• KWS SAAT SE & Co. KGaA (OTCPK:KNKZF): FY GAAP EPS of €4.24.
  • Revenue of €1.68B (flat Y/Y).
